title: The Memory Code — rereading
body_markdown:
## The Memory Code (Lynne Kelly) — rereading

Kelly's thesis: oral cultures encoded enormous amounts of knowledge
through memory palaces tied to landscape. *Everything* was a mnemonic
— songlines, ceremonies, carved posts, dance.

Key takeaways from this pass:

1. The method of loci isn't a trick — it's the default mode of
   preliterate cognition.
2. Encoding works best when the memorized material is *performed*,
   not just recalled silently.
3. Landscape-based palaces are more durable than constructed ones.

The application to LLM-backed wikis is obvious: the wiki *is* the
palace, and the entities are the stations.
